summary: Set 030 Session 1: shipped read-side schema v3 foundation. Python helper (ai_router/progress.py) and TypeScript mirror (tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/progress.ts) with get_progress(), synthesize_v3_from_v2(), validate_invariants() enforcing the 8 v3 rules. Schema doc fully rewritten for v3 (shape, derived values, invariants, Lightweight one-field-flip worked example, migration recipe). Example JSON/MD updated to v3 closed shape with v2-vs-v3 narrative. Proposal doc lingering 'done' references patched to 'complete' (D2 terminology lock). Router-config registers spec-title-extraction task type (D14: Session 5 fallback dependency removed). Round A verification (gpt-5-4, $0.275525) found 6 must-fix issues; all addressed (force-promote removed; strict-int filtering; contiguous-from-1; session-level cancelled rejected; rule 8 always-fires; rule-2 label for unknown top status). Tests: 484 pytest passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+8 vs pre-S1); 46/46 mocha in new progress.test.ts; TypeScript type-check clean.

reason.specifics: Session 2 ships the Phase 2 dual-write writers (register_session_start + close_session emit BOTH v3 sessions[] and the legacy progress triple derived from it). Session 1's read-side helpers + invariants are the contract those writers must satisfy; keeping the same orchestrator preserves the architectural context of how get_progress() drives the dual-write parity test and how SessionStateInvariantError is raised on writer-side rule violations.
